---
# =====================================================================
#  Policy brief — one-pager (LaTeX/PDF via Quarto)
#  Render:  quarto render research-highlights-template.qmd
#  Design system: _style.tex (colours, fonts, boxes, macros)
# =====================================================================
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: pdflatex
    documentclass: article
    fontsize: 10pt
    geometry:
      - top=0.32in
      - bottom=0.32in
      - left=0.583in
      - right=0.583in
    include-in-header: ../_style.tex
    keep-tex: true
---

```{=latex}
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% MASTHEAD
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\briefheader{%
  \includegraphics[height=0.45in]{../logos/eil-logo-maroon.png}%
}{Research Highlights}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% TITLE + CITATION
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\brieftitle{[Title of the brief]}
\briefcitation{[Author(s)] \textperiodcentered{} [Journal Name], [Year]}

\vspace{3pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% BOTTOM LINE  (~50 words)
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\begin{bottomline}
\textbf{[One-sentence statement of the core finding in bold.]} [One or two sentences expanding on the finding and its significance.]
\end{bottomline}

\vspace{2pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% BACKGROUND  (~90 words)
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\sectionhead{Background}
\noindent [Two to three sentences providing context for the research question. What is the policy problem? Why does it matter? What did researchers want to know?]

\vspace{4pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% TWO COLUMNS  (~50 words each)
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent
\begin{minipage}[t]{0.483\linewidth}
\subhead{[Column 1 heading]}
{\colsize [Two to four sentences on the first supporting theme — e.g., why the topic matters, relevant background, prior evidence.]\par}
\end{minipage}\hfill
\begin{minipage}[t]{0.483\linewidth}
\subhead{[Column 2 heading]}
{\colsize [Two to four sentences on the second supporting theme — e.g., a gap in knowledge, the case for evaluation, or a contrasting perspective.]\par}
\end{minipage}

\vspace{12pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% PULL QUOTE | FIGURE  (quote: ~45 words)
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent
\begin{minipage}[t]{0.40\linewidth}
\vspace{0pt}
\begin{pullquote}
[Two to three sentences summarizing the key result in plain language. Include a \quotelink{short highlighted phrase} that captures the headline finding.]
\end{pullquote}
\end{minipage}\hfill
\begin{minipage}[t]{0.575\linewidth}
\vspace{0pt}
\figtitle{Fig.\ 1 \textperiodcentered{} [Figure title]}{Source: [Author(s)] ([Year]).}
\fbox{\parbox[c][2.0in][c]{\dimexpr\linewidth-2\fboxsep-2\fboxrule}{\centering\color{muted}\small [Figure placeholder]}}
\end{minipage}

\vspace{0pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% IMPLICATIONS  (~25–35 words each)
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\sectionhead{Implications}

\noindent
\begin{minipage}[t]{0.310\linewidth}
\colhead{[Implication 1]}
{\impcolsize [Two to three sentences on the first policy implication. Use \impllink{a linked phrase} to highlight a key takeaway.]\par}
\end{minipage}\hfill
\begin{minipage}[t]{0.310\linewidth}
\colhead{[Implication 2]}
{\impcolsize [Two to three sentences on the second policy implication. Use \impllink{a linked phrase} to highlight a key takeaway.]\par}
\end{minipage}\hfill
\begin{minipage}[t]{0.310\linewidth}
\colhead{[Implication 3]}
{\impcolsize [Two to three sentences on the third policy implication. Use \impllink{a linked phrase} to highlight a key takeaway.]\par}
\end{minipage}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% FOOTER  (~10–15 words)
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\brieffooter{Method: [One sentence describing the research design.] Full paper: [doi.org/...]}
```
